steps.text: Keep the other Party generally informed of its relevant plans and schedules
Respond to the other Party's requests for information to the extent reasonable and pract

Keep the other Party generally informed of its relevant plans and schedules, respond to the other Party's requests for information, and strive to recognize and minimize problems arising from regulatory activities.
Make staff members of the Parties readily available for collaboration, information sharing, and resolution of concerns.
Work in good faith to resolve concerns in a timely manner.
Treat the drafts of any proposed or final rules, including any preambles thereof, and any other related documents or information, as pre-decisional and deliberative information.
Formulate standards of general application intended to control radiological hazards in the form of limits on radiation exposures or levels, or concentrations or quantities of radioactive material.
Provide for control of non-radiological hazards in standards of general application.

preconditions.text: EPA chooses to promulgate standards of general application under its authority in AEA § 275

constraints.text: Avoid undue delays that could adversely impact each Party's ability to carry out its statutory responsibilities under AEA §§ 84 and 275.

Neither Party will disclose or transmit the draft regulations or other related documents or information to any third party unless expressly agreed to by the other Party or as required by applicable law.

steps.text: Consult with the NRC.
Promulgate standards of general application.
Promulgate regulations for regulating ISR activities.

preconditions.text: The EPA must consult with the NRC before the promulgation of any standards of general application.

Until such time as the EPA promulgates new standards of general application under AEA § 275, the existing groundwater protection standards currently set forth in 40 CFR must be used for current regulatory activities at ISR facilities and for any NRC rulemaking concerning ISR activities.

constraints.text: Standards of general application intended to control non-radiological hazards must provide for the protection of human health and the environment consistent with the standards required under subtitle C of the Solid Waste Disposal Act (SWDA).

Standards of general application must not direct the use of a particular, detailed management, engineering, or technical method or test to demonstrate licensee compliance, nor restrict the regulatory agency's selection of an appropriate methodology or test to monitor licensee compliance.

steps.text: NRC requests the concurrence of the EPA, in accordance with AEA § 84a.(3), that general requirements for the management of byproduct material are at least comparable to requirements applicable to the possession, transfer, and disposal of similar hazardous material regulated by the EPA under the SWDA.

The Director, Office of Nuclear Material Safety and Safeguards, sends the request in writing to the EPA through the Director, Office of Radiation and Indoor Air, including a copy of the NRC's draft final rule.
The Parties will make all [efforts to resolve the disagreement].

preconditions.text: An NRC rulemaking would promulgate general requirements for the management of byproduct material.
A disagreement arises between the Parties concerning the EPA's concurrence regarding the NRC's draft final rule containing the general requirements for the management of byproduct material.

constraints.text: The SWDA, as amended, excludes 'byproduct material' as defined by the AEA, from its definition of 'solid waste' (42 U.S.C. § 6903(27)).

steps.text: Make all reasonable efforts to resolve any disagreement concerning the EPA's concurrence regarding the NRC's draft final rule as collegially and quickly as possible.
Raise the resolution of the disagreement to the Office of the Executive Director for Operations for the NRC and to the Assistant Administrator for Air and Radiation for the EPA.

preconditions.text: A disagreement arises between the Parties concerning the EPA's concurrence regarding the NRC's draft final rule containing the general requirements for the management of byproduct material.
Resolution is necessary (i.e., initial collegial efforts do not resolve the disagreement).

constraints.text: The Parties will endeavor to resolve all disagreements prior to publication of the final rule in the Federal Register.
All commitments made by the EPA and the NRC in this MOU are subject to the availability of appropriated funds and budget priorities.

steps.text: Designate a representative and alternate representative, as described in Attachment A, to ensure coordination between the EPA and the NRC.
Change representative by providing written notice to the other Party.
Extend or amend the MOU through the mutual written agreement of the Parties.
Terminate participation in this MOU.
Execute the MOU by signing and dating on the signature page.

preconditions.text: Provide written notice to the other Party at least 30 days in advance of the desired termination date.

constraints.text: This MOU does not obligate any funding.
